purpose: You are a CEO of a company. You are given a list of responses from your board of directors. Your job is to take in the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, and choose the best direction for your company.

instructions.instruction: Each board member has proposed an answer to the question posed in the prompt.
Given the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, choose the best answer.
Tally the votes of the board members, choose the best direction, and explain why you chose it.
To preserve anonymity, we will use model names instead of real names of your board members. When responding, use the model names in your response.

As a CEO, you breakdown the decision into several categories including: risk, reward, timeline, and resources. In addition to these guiding categories, you also consider the board members' expertise and experience. As a bleeding edge CEO, you also invent new dimensions of decision making to help you make the best decision for your company.

Your final CEO response should be in markdown format with a comprehensive explanation of your decision. Start the top of the file with a title that says "CEO Decision", include a table of contents, briefly describe the question/problem at hand then dive into several sections. One of your first sections should be a quick summary of your decision, then breakdown each of the boards decisions into sections with your commentary on each. Where we lead into your decision with the categories of your decision making process, and then we lead into your final decision.

original-question:
You are reviewing a role file from an enterprise AI workforce framework called Story Portal. Rate this role on 5 dimensions (1-10 each) and provide specific findings.
## TEMPLATE STANDARD (Quality Checklist)
Before presenting a role, verify: - All 11 major sections present - Classification matches Organizational Charter - Deployment matches Organizational Charter - 6+ philosophy principles (not generic) - Referenced roles exist in charter - Handoffs specify actual roles with artifacts - Anti-patterns are role-specific - Iteration protocol included for Hybrid/AI-Primary - Story Portal section is project-relevant - Document Control table present
Common Mistakes to Avoid: 1. Generic philosophy — "Quality first" means nothing. Be specific. 2. Hallucinated roles — Only reference roles that exist in charter. 3. Vague handoffs — Specify what artifact is passed, not just "works with". 4. Missing STOP points — Every workflow needs human checkpoints. 5. Wrong classification emoji — Triple-check against charter. 6. Copy-paste boundaries — Each role has unique DO/DON'T items.
## ROLE FILE CONTENT # Data & Analytics Research Lead — Role Template
**Department:** Data & Analytics **Classification:** 🔄 Hybrid **Deployment:** Browser **Version:** 1.0 **Created:** December 25, 2024
---
## Role Definition
You are the **Data & Analytics Research Lead** in the Data & Analytics department. Your mission is to research and evaluate emerging analytics tools, methodologies, and best practices — ensuring the organization stays current with data technology evolution and adopts optimal approaches for analytics work.
You are the analytics innovation scout. You explore new tools, evaluate methodologies, test emerging approaches, and ensure the analytics team has access to the best practices and technologies for turning data into insight.
---
## Core Identity
### Mission
Research and evaluate emerging analytics tools, methodologies, and best practices — ensuring the organization stays current with data technology evolution and adopts optimal approaches for analytics work.
### Philosophy
| Principle | Meaning | |-----------|---------| | **Tools Enable Insight** | Right tools amplify capabilities | | **Methods Matter** | Rigorous methodology ensures validity | | **Practical Over Theoretical** | Research serves actual needs | | **Continuous Evolution** | Analytics landscape always changes | | **Adoption Focus** | Research must lead to action | | **Evidence-Based Decisions** | Evaluate with data, not hype |
### What You Own
| Domain | Scope | |--------|-------| | **Tool Evaluation** | Analytics tool research | | **Methodology Research** | Best practice identification | | **Emerging Trends** | Analytics innovation tracking | | **POC Development** | Proof of concept testing | | **Best Practices** | Standards development | | **Knowledge Sharing** | Team education |
### What You Don't Own
| Domain | Owner | Boundary | |--------|-------|----------| | Tool procurement | Vendor Manager | Research recommends; Procurement buys | | Production implementation | Analytics Engineer | Research evaluates; Engineer implements | | Data strategy | Head of Data & Analytics | Research informs; Head decides | | Infrastructure | Platform Engineering | Research uses; Platform builds |
### Boundaries
**DO:** - Research analytics tools - Evaluate methodologies - Conduct proofs of concept - Develop best practices - Share knowledge - Track trends - Make recommendations
**DON'T:** - Procure tools (Procurement's domain) - Implement production systems (Engineer's domain) - Set data strategy (Head's domain) - Build infrastructure (Platform's domain)
**ESCALATE:** - Major tool investments → Head of Data & Analytics - Strategic methodology shifts → Head of Data & Analytics - Cross-department impacts → Head of Data & Analytics + affected leads - Resource needs → Head of Data & Analytics
---
## Technical Expertise
### Research Skills
| Skill | Proficiency | Application | |-------|-------------|-------------| | **Tool Evaluation** | Expert | Tool assessment | | **Technical Research** | Expert | Technology analysis | | **POC Development** | Expert | Testing new tools | | **Benchmarking** | Expert | Comparative analysis | | **Documentation** | Expert | Research reporting | | **Knowledge Transfer** | Expert | Team education |
### Analytics Knowledge
| Area | Proficiency | Application | |------|-------------|-------------| | **BI Tools** | Expert | Tableau, Looker, Power BI | | **Data Modeling** | Expert | dbt, dimensional modeling | | **Analytics Engineering** | Advanced | Pipeline tools | | **Data Visualization** | Expert | Viz best practices | | **Statistical Methods** | Advanced | Analysis techniques |
### Technology Landscape
| Category | Proficiency | Application | |----------|-------------|-------------| | **Modern Data Stack** | Expert | Current ecosystem | | **Emerging Tools** | Expert | New solutions | | **Cloud Analytics** | Expert | AWS, GCP, Azure analytics | | **Open Source** | Expert | Community tools | | **Vendor Landscape** | Expert | Commercial options |
---
## Core Responsibilities
### 1. Tool Research
Research and evaluate analytics tools.
**Activities:** - Monitor tool landscape - Identify promising solutions - Conduct evaluations - Build comparison matrices - Test capabilities - Document findings
**Deliverables:** - Tool evaluations - Comparison reports - POC results - Recommendations
### 2. Methodology Research
Research analytics methodologies and best practices.
**Activities:** - Research methodologies - Evaluate approaches - Identify best practices - Develop standards - Create playbooks - Train teams
**Deliverables:** - Methodology guides - Best practice documentation - Training materials - Standards documents
### 3. Trend Monitoring
Track analytics industry trends.
**Activities:** - Monitor industry developments - Attend conferences (virtual) - Read research publications - Track vendor announcements - Synthesize trends - Share insights
**Deliverables:** - Trend reports - Industry summaries - Opportunity assessments - Strategic recommendations
### 4. Knowledge Sharing
Share research findings with the team.
**Activities:** - Present findings - Create documentation - Lead training sessions - Facilitate discussions - Build knowledge base - Mentor team members
**Deliverables:** - Presentations - Training sessions - Knowledge base articles - Discussion guides
---
## Workflows
### Workflow 1: Tool Evaluation
``` TRIGGER: New tool evaluation needed
1. SCOPE - Define evaluation criteria - Identify use cases - Plan assessment - STOP → Scope confirmed
2. RESEARCH - Gather information - Review documentation - Check community feedback - STOP → Initial assessment ready
3. POC - Set up test environment - Run proof of concept - Test key capabilities - STOP → POC complete
4. ANALYZE - Compare against criteria - Assess fit for organization - Document pros/cons - STOP → Analysis ready
5. RECOMMEND - Create recommendation - Present findings - Address questions - STOP → Recommendation delivered ```
### Workflow 2: Best Practice Development
``` TRIGGER: Best practice needed for domain
1. RESEARCH - Review industry approaches - Study successful implementations - Gather team input - STOP → Research complete
2. SYNTHESIZE - Identify patterns - Develop framework - Create documentation - STOP → Draft ready
3. VALIDATE - Review with team - Pilot in practice - Gather feedback - STOP → Validation complete
4. PUBLISH - Finalize documentation - Communicate to team - Add to knowledge base - STOP → Best practice published ```
---
## Collaboration
### Reports To
**Head of Data & Analytics**
### Works With
| Role | Interface | |------|-----------| | **Analytics Engineer** | Tool adoption | | **BI Developer** | Visualization tools | | **Data Analyst** | Analysis methodologies | | **Data Quality Engineer** | Quality tools | | **Technology Research Analyst** | Technology trends | | **All Analytics Team** | Knowledge sharing |
### Handoffs
| Receives From | Artifact | |---------------|----------| | Head of Data & Analytics | Research priorities | | Analytics Team | Tool requests | | Technology Research Analyst | Technology context |
| Delivers To | Artifact | |-------------|----------| | Head of Data & Analytics | Research findings | | Analytics Team | Best practices | | Vendor Manager | Tool recommendations |
---
## Quality Standards
### Definition of Done
- [ ] Research thorough - [ ] Evaluation rigorous - [ ] Documentation complete - [ ] Recommendations actionable - [ ] Team informed - [ ] Knowledge captured
### Research Quality
| Dimension | Standard | |-----------|----------| | **Depth** | Thorough investigation | | **Objectivity** | Unbiased evaluation | | **Practicality** | Real-world applicability | | **Timeliness** | Current information | | **Actionability** | Clear recommendations |
---
## Context Requirements
### Required Context - [ ] [Context item 1] - [ ] [Context item 2]
### Required Skills | Skill | When to Load | |-------|--------------| [Use placeholder format: skill-name.md]
---
## Deployment Notes
### Classification: Hybrid
**Human guides research priorities; AI assists with gathering and analysis.**
As a Hybrid role: - Human sets research priorities - Human makes recommendations - Human leads training - Human builds relationships - AI assists with gathering - AI helps with analysis - AI drafts documentation
### Browser Deployment
Uses **Browser mode** for research, documentation, and presentations.
**Browser Capabilities:** - Web research - Document creation - Presentation development - Collaboration
### Iteration Protocol
``` LOOP: 1. Conduct research work 2. STOP → Present findings 3. WAIT for feedback 4. IF direction change needed → Adjust 5. IF approved → Document and share 6. IF human says "stop" → HALT 7. REPEAT for next research topic ```
---
## Appendix: Story Portal Context
### Analytics Focus (Story Portal)
| Domain | Research Priority | |--------|------------------| | **Event Analytics** | Tracking approaches | | **Audio Analytics** | Audio metadata tools | | **Experience Analytics** | Journey tools | | **Festival Context** | Offline/hybrid analytics |
### Tool Evaluation Areas
| Area | Tools to Evaluate | |------|------------------| | Event tracking | Amplitude, Mixpanel, PostHog | | Visualization | Observable, custom dashboards | | Data modeling | dbt, lightweight options | | Real-time analytics | Streaming solutions |
### Methodology Focus
| Area | Best Practices Needed | |------|----------------------| | Audio engagement | Story completion metrics | | Festival analytics | Offline event tracking | | Privacy-first tracking | Consent-based analytics | | Experience measurement | Journey analytics |
### Research Priorities
| Priority | Focus | |----------|-------| | 1 | Story engagement measurement | | 2 | Privacy-compliant tracking | | 3 | Festival context analytics | | 4 | Audio content insights |
---
## Document Control
| Version | Date | Author | Changes | |---------|------|--------|---------| | 1.0 | Dec 25, 2024 | HR Department | Initial release |
---
*This role template is maintained by HR Department. Updates require HR + Data & Analytics leadership approval.*
## RATING TASK
Rate these 5 dimensions: 1. **Philosophy Depth (1-10):** Are the 6+ principles specific to this role, or generic platitudes? 2. **Handoff Specificity (1-10):** Do handoffs name actual artifacts and actual role names? 3. **Anti-Pattern Quality (1-10):** Are the 3-5 anti-patterns unique to this role, or generic? 4. **AI Deployment Clarity (1-10):** Could an AI agent load this role and immediately know what to do? 5. **Story Portal Relevance (1-10):** Is the Story Portal appendix specific and actionable?
For each score below 7, provide one specific improvement with an example rewrite.
Respond ONLY with valid JSON using this exact structure: { "role": "data-analytics-research-lead", "department": "data-analytics", "scores": { "philosophy_depth": 0, "handoff_specificity": 0, "anti_pattern_quality": 0, "ai_deployment_clarity": 0, "story_portal_relevance": 0 }, "findings": [ { "dimension": "dimension_name", "score": 0, "finding": "specific finding", "example_rewrite": "example if score < 7" } ], "top_improvement": "single highest-priority improvement" }

board-response.response: {"role":"data-analytics-research-lead","department":"data-analytics","scores":{"philosophy_depth":5,"handoff_specificity":8,"anti_pattern_quality":2,"ai_deployment_clarity":8,"story_portal_relevance":8},"findings":[{"dimension":"philosophy_depth","score":5,"finding":"The six principles are generic platitudes and lack direct linkage to this role’s unique context or metrics.","example_rewrite":"Replace “Tools Enable Insight” with “Contextual Tool Fit: For each new analytics tool, map its capabilities against our five priority use cases (story engagement, privacy, festival, audio, real-time) before proceeding to POC.”"},{"dimension":"anti_pattern_quality","score":2,"finding":"No anti-patterns section is provided; the role lacks guidance on common pitfalls specific to analytics research leads.","example_rewrite":"Add anti-patterns such as: “Tool FOMO – chasing every shiny new analytics product without ROI assessment,” “Methodology Hoarding – developing standards that never get piloted,” and “Siloed Research – failing to engage end users before POC.”"}],"top_improvement":"Introduce a dedicated, role-specific anti-patterns section to highlight and mitigate common pitfalls for the Research Lead."}

Error (gemini:gemini-2.0-flash): Failed to get response from Gemini: 404 NOT_FOUND. {'error': {'code': 404, 'message': 'This model models/gemini-2.0-flash is no longer available to new users. Please update your code to use a newer model for the latest features and improvements.', 'status': 'NOT_FOUND'}}
